footer.tagline: The fastest first step toward a web for everyone

landing.heroEyebrow: Automated web accessibility audits & remediation guides
landing.heroDesc: Enter one URL — we collect representative pages, audit them against WCAG 2.2 and KWCAG 2.2, and produce an audit report with practical remediation guides.
landing.featuresTitle: From audit to remediation, in one place

features.autoDesc: The axe-core engine collects representative pages and checks ~90 rules automatically.
features.kwcagDesc: See WCAG 2.2 and all 33 KWCAG 2.2 checkpoints together in one report.
features.reportDesc: Review violations with code locations and remediation guides, then download as PDF.
features.manualDesc: We never hide what automation can't judge — each manual checkpoint ships with testing instructions.

landing.how1Desc: Sign in and enter your site address; representative pages are collected automatically.
landing.how2Desc: A headless browser opens each page and audits it against WCAG · KWCAG.
landing.how3Desc: Compliance rate, checkpoint matrix, and remediation guides — on the web and in PDF.
landing.honestyDesc: Automated tools can only find part of all accessibility issues. A11y Check states its limits clearly and guides you through the checks that require a human.
landing.openSourceTitle: Built in the open on GitHub
landing.openSourceDesc: The audit engine and rule catalog are developed on GitHub. Contributions to the guides are welcome.

demo.title: Web Accessibility Audit Report
demo.sampleCaption: The card above is an illustrative sample. Running an audit produces a real report in this format.

landing.ctaDemoReport: See a real sample report (our own audit)

teaser.title: Check one page free — right now
teaser.desc: No sign-in needed. Paste a page URL and get an instant accessibility check. (Twice a day)
teaser.loadingNote: Analysing the page — this takes about 10–30 seconds.
teaser.notSaved: The result screen is not saved and cannot be viewed again. Only the checked domain and summary figures are collected for service statistics.
teaser.cachedNote: This page was checked recently, so we showed the result instantly.
teaser.noViolations: No violations found by automated checks. (Automated tools cover only part of the criteria — manual review is recommended.)
teaser.moreNodes: Locations of the remaining {count} are available after sign-up
teaser.lockedTitle: Go deeper with a free account
teaser.lockedFeatures: Site-wide scans · KWCAG 33-item matrix · manual review workflow · certification readiness · PDF/CSV export · scheduled scans

errors.invalidUrl: This address can't be checked. Enter a public web page URL including https://.
errors.captcha: Bot check failed. Please try again shortly.
errors.globalCap: Today's free checks are all used up.
errors.robots: This site disallows automated checks via robots.txt.
errors.scanFailed: Couldn't check the page. Verify the address or try again shortly.
errors.unavailable: The free check is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly.

categories.title: What we check

keyboard.title: Keyboard & focus
keyboard.desc: Focus order/visibility, skip links, positive tabindex

structure.title: Structure & landmarks
structure.desc: Heading hierarchy, landmarks, tables, reading order

language.title: Language
language.desc: Page lang, language of parts, valid codes

wcag22.title: WCAG 2.2 additions
wcag22.desc: Target size (24px), focus not obscured, and more

categories.caption: 106 automated rules detect 30 of the 55 WCAG 2.2 A/AA success criteria (54.5%). Our manual review workflow guides you through the rest — no automated tool alone can guarantee conformance.

login.title: Sign in
login.desc: Continue with a social account or your email.
login.privacy: We only collect your profile (name, email) to manage your audit history.
login.sent: We sent a sign-in link to your email. Check your inbox (and spam folder).
login.sendFailed: Failed to send. Please try again shortly.
login.errorConfirm: The link has expired or was already used. Please request a new one.
login.forgotDesc: Enter your email and we'll send a reset link.
login.signUpSent: We sent a confirmation email. Click the link (check spam too) to finish signing up.
login.resetSent: We sent a reset link to your email. Please check your inbox.
login.errPasswordShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
login.errNotConfirmed: Your email isn't confirmed yet. Click the link in your inbox first.
login.errExists: This email is already registered. Sign in or reset your password.
login.errGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again shortly.

scanPage.title: Run Audit
scanPage.desc: Enter the site URL and choose how to build the sample. The audit runs against WCAG 2.2 and KWCAG 2.2 and produces a report.
scanPage.modeAutoDesc: Builds a representative sample from the sitemap and internal links (WCAG-EM structured + random sample).
scanPage.modeManualDesc: You specify the exact pages to audit. Only pages on the same site (same domain) are allowed.
scanPage.manualOriginHint: Only pages on the same domain as the audit URL are allowed; extra lines beyond the limit are dropped.
scanPage.accessCheckLink: Does the site block bots? Check auditability →
scanPage.manualOverLimit: You entered more pages than this domain's limit ({max}). Please reduce to {max} or fewer.
scanPage.manualVerifyHint: Verifying domain ownership raises the limit to {verified} pages.
scanPage.manualHostMismatch: These pages are on a different host than the audit URL ({host}) and cannot be scanned. Remove or fix these lines.

apiErrors.quota_daily: You have used all of your daily scan quota ({limit}).
apiErrors.quota_weekly: You have used all of your weekly scan quota ({limit}).
apiErrors.quota_monthly: You have used all of your monthly scan quota ({limit}).
apiErrors.concurrent: A scan is already in progress. Please try again after it finishes.
apiErrors.manualOverLimit: You entered {count} pages, but this domain's limit is {limit} pages.
apiErrors.manualOverLimitVerify: You entered {count} pages, but this domain's limit is {limit} pages. Verify domain ownership to scan more pages.
apiErrors.createFailed: Failed to create the scan. Please try again shortly.
apiErrors.invalidInput: Please check the URL to scan.
apiErrors.url_invalid_url: That doesn't look like a valid URL.
apiErrors.url_bad_scheme: Only http/https addresses can be scanned.
apiErrors.url_private_address: Private/internal network addresses cannot be scanned.
apiErrors.url_resolve_failed: Host not found. Please check the address.
apiErrors.url_fetch_failed: Could not reach the target. Please try again shortly.
apiErrors.pageOtherDomain: A page is on a different domain or invalid: {url}
apiErrors.pagesEmpty: Please enter at least one page to scan.
apiErrors.pdfNotDone: Only completed reports can be downloaded as PDF.
apiErrors.pdfFailed: Failed to generate the PDF. Please try again shortly.
apiErrors.rateLimited: Too many requests. Please try again later.
apiErrors.checkFailed: The check failed. Please try again shortly.

accessCheck.title: Bot-block checker
accessCheck.desc: Diagnose whether and how a site blocks automated audit bots. Does not use your audit quota.
accessCheck.failed: Diagnosis failed. Please try again shortly.

ok.label: ✓ Automated audit possible
ok.desc: No bot blocking detected. You can run the automated audit right away.
ok.solution: No action needed — start your audit.

robots-blocked.label: Blocked by robots.txt
robots-blocked.desc: The site's robots.txt disallows our audit bot. A11y Check respects robots.txt, so automated audits will not run in this state.
robots-blocked.solution: If you manage the site, add an allow rule for a11ychk-bot (guide #1 below). Otherwise, audit with the Chrome extension.

ua-blocked.label: Bot User-Agent blocked
ua-blocked.desc: Regular browsers connect fine, but the audit bot's User-Agent is blocked — a server or WAF UA filter.
ua-blocked.solution: Ask the site admin to allow the a11ychk-bot User-Agent (guide #2). For an immediate audit, use the Chrome extension.

challenge.label: Bot-defense challenge
challenge.desc: A bot-defense service (e.g. Cloudflare) is blocking all automated access. Allowing the UA alone may not be enough.
challenge.solution: Add an exception for a11ychk-bot in the security service, or audit with the Chrome extension — it runs in your real browser, unaffected by bot defenses.

http-error.label: HTTP error
http-error.desc: The page returns an error regardless of bot blocking. Check the address and whether the site is up.
http-error.solution: Verify the URL and try again shortly.

unreachable.label: Unreachable
unreachable.desc: Could not connect to the site — wrong address, DNS issue, or temporary outage.
unreachable.solution: Verify the URL and try again shortly.

accessCheck.extensionCta: Audit with the Chrome extension → (works despite bot blocking / login)
accessCheck.guideDesc: Recommended solutions by block type. The Chrome extension always works.

robots.title: Allow the audit bot in robots.txt
robots.desc: If you manage the site, add the rule below. A11y Check is a well-behaved bot that respects robots.txt, so an allow rule is all it takes.

waf.title: Allow the User-Agent in your WAF/CDN
waf.desc: If you use Cloudflare, AWS WAF, etc., add a skip/allow rule for the audit bot UA. In Cloudflare: Security → WAF → Custom rules with a User-Agent condition.

extension.title: Audit with the Chrome extension (most reliable)
extension.desc: The extension runs inside your real browser, so it audits any page regardless of bot blocking, login, or intranet restrictions. Results are saved to your account.

staging.title: Audit a staging environment
staging.desc: If you can't add security exceptions in production, audit a staging/dev URL without bot defenses. Note it must be reachable from the public internet — private IPs cannot be audited.

scanCta.title: Start a new audit
scanCta.desc: On the audit page, build the sample automatically or enter pages manually, then start the audit.

scanForm.label: Page URL to audit
scanForm.targetHint: The WCAG-EM Step 1 target level. Most audits use AA. Defaults to AA if left blank.
scanForm.notesPlaceholder: Purpose, special considerations, etc. (recorded in the report)
scanForm.hint: Only public pages can be audited. Verified domains get up to 10 pages; others get 5.
scanForm.extensionLink: Audit pages behind login with the Chrome extension →

domains.title: My domains
domains.deleteConfirm: Delete {host}? Ownership verification, public listing, and badge settings will be removed.
domains.empty: No domains yet. Register and verify a domain to audit more pages per scan.
domains.verifyFailed: Verification failed. DNS/deploy changes can take a few minutes.
domains.listedHint: List your verified domain publicly and issue a clickable badge.
domains.badgeNotice: A transparency badge based on automated checks — not an official certification. Listing exposes the site name and audit result in the public directory.
domains.verifyIntro: This step confirms the site is yours. Pick whichever of the 3 methods below is easiest for you.
domains.verifyOnlyOne: ✅ You don't need to do all three — any ONE of them verifies your domain.
domains.verifyFileDesc: Create a text file with the 'content' below and upload it to the 'path' below on your site. You can upload it via your site builder or your hosting file manager.
domains.verifyMetaTitle: Option 2 · Add one line to your homepage
domains.verifyMetaDesc: Paste the line below inside the <head> of your homepage. (Convenient if you can edit your homepage directly.)
domains.verifyDnsDesc: In the DNS settings where you bought your domain (Cloudflare, GoDaddy, etc.), add the TXT record below. (Recommended if you're comfortable with DNS.)
domains.verifyAfter: After setting it up, click the 'Verify' button above. It may take a few minutes to take effect.
domains.verifyFailedHint: DNS records can take a few minutes to propagate. Please try again shortly. The file and meta-tag methods usually take effect immediately.
domains.verifyError: We could not process the request. Please try again in a moment.
domains.verifyLimitDesc: You have verified the maximum number of sites for your current tier. To register more sites, please contact the administrator.
domains.cfDesc: If your domain is managed on Cloudflare, we can create the DNS (TXT) verification record for you using an API token.
domains.cfTokenHelp: A token with Zone · DNS edit permission is required. The token is not stored and is used only for this request.
domains.cfDone: DNS record created. After it propagates (usually 1–2 minutes), press “Verify” above to finish.
domains.cfZoneNotFound: No matching Cloudflare zone was found for this domain. Make sure the token includes permission for this domain.
domains.cfAuthError: The token is invalid or lacks permission. Please check Zone · DNS edit permission.
domains.cfApiError: An error occurred during Cloudflare setup. Please try again in a moment.
domains.cfInvalid: Please enter a valid API token.
domains.scheduleExplain: Scheduled scans run automatically around 3 AM KST each day, checking only domains whose interval has elapsed. A specific time of day is not configurable.
domains.publicHintOn: Listed in the public directory; the badge links to this report.
domains.publicHintOff: Private — the badge image works, but no link or directory listing. Switching to private also invalidates existing public links.
domains.settingFailed: Could not save. Please try again.
domains.addInvalid: Not a valid domain. Example: example.com
domains.copyFailed: Copy failed. Please select and copy the code manually.
domains.itemChangesTitle: Changes by item (vs previous scan, violating elements)
domains.rulesHint: Exclude rules you consider false positives from this domain's automated scans. Applies from the next scan; reports state that rules were excluded.
domains.rulesNote: Use exclusions carefully — hiding real violations makes the compliance rate diverge from reality. Up to 20 rules.

recent.title: Recent audits
recent.empty: No audits yet — enter a site URL and get your first accessibility report in a minute or two.

overview.title: Domain overview
overview.desc: Latest result for each domain you have scanned.

scan.title: Audit progress
scan.runningDesc: Collecting representative pages and running the automated audit. This takes about 1–3 minutes.

queued.title: Waiting in queue
queued.desc: Many scans are running right now, so your scan has been queued. It will start automatically when its turn comes.

score.title: Automated compliance rate
score.desc: Share of automatically checkable rules that passed. This measures automated coverage, not overall accessibility.

scores.combinedDesc: Final rate integrating automated checks and reviewer judgments.
scores.automatedDesc: Automatically determined by axe, custom rules, and site-level checks.
scores.manualDesc: Judgments entered by the reviewer after manual inspection.
scores.legend: Combined uses the reviewer's judgment for each criterion when present, otherwise the automated result.

metaForm.hint: This information appears on the report cover, print output, and the WCAG-EM Report Tool export.
metaForm.title: Report title
metaForm.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Codeslog Web Accessibility Evaluation Report
metaForm.executiveSummaryPlaceholder: Write an overall assessment. Shown at the top of the printed report.
metaForm.failed: Failed to save. Please try again shortly.

review.notePlaceholder: Record how you tested and what you found.

pages.title: Sample pages
pages.summary: {done} of {total} sample pages audited; {failed} failed. Failed pages usually succeed on a re-run — see the reasons below.

em.repOk: The random sample surfaced no violation types absent from the structured sample. The sample is sufficiently representative.
em.repNew: The random sample surfaced {count} additional violation rule(s) not in the structured sample. A broader sample may be warranted for the full site.
em.statement: This report is an automated evaluation structured per WCAG-EM (Website Accessibility Conformance Evaluation Methodology), targeting WCAG 2.2 level {target}. It covers only what automated testing can judge and excludes items marked 'manual check' and multi-step processes. A complete WCAG 2.2 conformance determination requires expert manual evaluation; this automated evaluation alone does not support a conformance claim.

wcag.title: WCAG 2.2 success criteria results
wcag.desc: WCAG-EM Step 4 — automated outcome per success criterion at the target level.
wcag.notCheckedNote: 'Manual check' means the success criterion cannot be judged automatically and requires human verification. Use the 'manual checks' section below and the Chrome extension.

kwcag.title: KWCAG 2.2 checkpoint matrix
kwcag.desc: Automated results for all 33 checkpoints of the Korean Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2.
kwcag.certNote: Page rate is the share of scanned pages with no violations for the item. Korean accessibility certification requires ≥95% per item; the figures here are page-level approximations from automated checks and may differ from the element-level calculation used in audits.

violations.title: Violations & remediation guides
violations.empty: No violations found by the automated audit. Please review the manual checkpoints.

manual.title: Checkpoints requiring manual testing
manual.desc: These checkpoints cannot be judged automatically — a human must verify them. Use the instructions below or get a professional audit.

failedPages.title: Pages that could not be audited
failedPages.desc: The following pages were excluded from this automated audit for the reasons below. Re-running the audit shortly usually resolves them.

reasons.timeout: The page did not finish loading within the time limit. This can happen with slow or heavy sites.
reasons.unreachable: The page could not be reached. The address may have changed or the connection was temporarily unstable.
reasons.blocked: The page could not be audited due to site policy (e.g. robots rules) or because it resolved to a private/internal address.
reasons.temporary: A temporary error occurred during the audit. Re-running usually resolves it.
reasons.engine: The audit engine hit a temporary problem. Please try again.
reasons.unknown: This page could not be audited for an unknown reason. Please try again.

report.disclaimer: This report is produced by an automated tool (axe-core) and does not guarantee overall accessibility compliance. Automated audits detect only a subset of issues; official certification requires expert manual review.

inline.reviewIntro: Automated tooling could not judge these rules. Verify them using the guidance below, then record your outcome via 'Review'.
inline.manualFallback: This success criterion is not covered by automated testing. See the W3C Understanding document for testing guidance.

rescan.failed: Rescan failed. Please try again shortly.

reviewData.title: Collected values for review
reviewData.desc: Automation confirmed these values exist; whether they are meaningful requires human judgment. Skim the collected values, then record pass/fail in the matrices above.
reviewData.altsHint: Check that each alt text actually conveys the image's meaning.
reviewData.labelsHint: Check that each label clearly says what to enter.
reviewData.genericLinksHint: Passes if the surrounding sentence or list clarifies the link's purpose.

compare.title: Change since previous audit
compare.desc: Compared with the audit on {date}. See the effect of your remediation work.

share.onDesc: Anyone with the link can view this report (read-only).
share.copyFailed: Copy failed. Please select the URL manually.

priority.title: Top fixes
priority.desc: Highest-impact violations by severity and scale. Fixing them in this order yields the biggest improvement.

notice.done: This report is filtered to items with a verdict. See the 'Everything' view for the full results including manual-check guidance.
notice.issues: This report is filtered to failing items only. See the 'Everything' view for full results including passes and manual checks.
notice.auto: This report is filtered to items judged by automated checks. See the 'Everything' view for full results including items that require manual review.

notice.wcag: This report is printed with WCAG 2.2 results only. Switch to “Both” to see the full results including KWCAG checkpoints.
notice.kwcag: This report is printed with KWCAG 2.2 results only. Switch to “Both” to see the full results including WCAG success criteria.

manualProgress.title: Manual review progress
manualProgress.desc: Share of items the automated tool could not decide that a reviewer has now judged.

cert.title: Certification readiness
cert.evaluated: average over {evaluated} of 33 checkpoints with evidence
cert.note: An approximation of the expert-review pass threshold used by Korea's web accessibility quality certification (average compliance of the 33 checkpoints ≥95%; 85–95% triggers a second review), based on automated results and reviewer judgments. Checkpoints without evidence are excluded, and certification uses element-level scoring that may differ. Certification also includes a separate user evaluation (100% task success across disability types).

report.savePublicHint: Applies the current display mode to visitors via the share link and badge.

blind.label: Blind review mode
blind.hint: Enter judgments with automated results hidden (for research/audit). Screen-only — does not affect saving, sharing, or PDF.
blind.notice: Blind review mode is on — automated outcomes, violation counts, and details are hidden, and the output range is fixed to 'All'. Turn it off after judging to reveal automated results.

viewerCta.title: This report was generated by A11y Check
viewerCta.desc: A11y Check is a free service that automatically audits web accessibility against WCAG 2.2 and KWCAG 2.2 with actionable remediation guides.

report.excludedRulesNote: {count} rule(s) were excluded from this scan per domain settings:

error.impersonation: Nicknames that could impersonate an administrator or operator are not allowed.
error.failed: Failed to save. Please try again shortly.

standard.label: Preferred standard in reports
standard.default: Default (Korean: KWCAG first · English: WCAG first)
standard.help: Which standard appears first in the “Both” view of reports. You can always switch to the other standard.

referral.title: Level up
referral.intro: Earn higher-tier benefits through invitations and activity.
referral.m1Desc: Reached when {goal} friends sign up via your link and run their first scan.
referral.m2Desc: After reaching Plus 1, verify a domain and publish a report to earn it.
referral.linkUnavailable: Couldn't prepare your invite link. Please refresh shortly.
referral.capNote: Up to 2 invitations count per day, {cap} in total.

suspectReason.same_ip: Signed up from the same network — needs review

appeal.label: Explanation
appeal.placeholder: e.g. This is a colleague in the same office — tell us the situation
appeal.submitted: Submitted. It will be reviewed by an admin.

referral.m2Reward: Reward · {daily}/day, {weekly}/week, {monthly}/month scans, {pages} pages/scan, {ext} extension/day, {verify} verified domains

inquiries.title: Support & feature requests
inquiries.desc: Tell us about problems or ideas. Replies appear on this page.

new.subject: Subject
new.successHint: Thank you — we appreciate it. Replies appear under “My inquiries” below.

error.rateLimited: Please try again in a little while.

errorHint.invalid: Subject must be 1–200 characters and details 1–5000 characters.
errorHint.rateLimited: You can submit up to 5 inquiries per 10 minutes. Inquiries you already submitted appear in the list below.
errorHint.failed: This may be temporary. Please try again shortly.

inquiries.anonTitle: Sign in to leave an inquiry
inquiries.anonDesc: Sign in to submit inquiries and receive replies. Bug reports and feature requests are also welcome via GitHub issues — no account needed.

admin.title: Admin console
admin.denied: This page is for administrators only.

plansControl.hint: While inactive, all users run on the free tier's limits and sample size. Activating makes assigned plans take effect.

users.title: User management
users.customHint: Leave blank to use the plan default; enter a number to set a per-user limit
users.plansInactive: Plan enforcement is off, so the plan assignments below have no effect — every user gets the free limit.
users.plansInactiveLink: Turn on enforcement in operations settings
users.bulkHint: Applies the selected plan to every user. Per-user custom limits are cleared. (Group management for future monetization)
users.pagesHint: Page limit: doubled for verified domains, max {max} pages

settings.title: Settings
settings.bulkPagesLabel: Bulk set page limit for all users
settings.bulkPagesHint: Sets the base page limit for every user (doubled for verified domains, max {max} pages). Apply with an empty value to revert to plan/default limits.

logs.title: Logs
logs.notMigrated: Log tables do not exist yet. Apply supabase/migrations/0006_admin_logs.sql in the Supabase SQL Editor.
logs.errorsNotMigrated: Error log table does not exist yet. Apply supabase/migrations/0008_app_errors.sql in the Supabase SQL Editor.

referrals.title: Referrals
referrals.intro: Review suspect invitations. Approving counts them as valid; rejecting also revokes the invitee's signup perk.

teaser.title: Teaser scan statistics
teaser.intro: Demand and quality metrics for the no-login teaser check. Only hostnames and summary figures are collected (no paths, IPs, or personal data); results are never shown to visitors again. Tables and averages cover the last 30 days.
teaser.empty: No teaser scans recorded yet. (Recording starts after migration 0026 is applied.)

guide.title: Manual testing guide
guide.desc: KWCAG 2.2 checkpoints that automation cannot judge, with instructions for testing them yourself. Identical to the 'manual checks' section of A11y Check reports.

extension.title: Connect Chrome extension
extension.desc: Install the A11y Check Chrome extension to audit pages behind login or the screen you're working on, and use the manual checklist. Connect the extension to your account on this page.
extension.step1: Install the 'A11y Check' extension from the Chrome Web Store.
extension.step2: Keep this page open and the extension connects to your account automatically.
extension.step3: Once connected, use 'Save results to my account' in the extension popup.
extension.waiting: Waiting for the extension to connect…
extension.notInstalled: If the extension isn't installed or recognized yet, install it and refresh this page.

errors.notFoundDesc: The address may have changed or the page was removed.
errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.desc: A temporary error kept this page from loading. Please try again in a moment.

about.title: About
about.intro: Enter a URL and A11y Check samples representative pages, audits them against WCAG 2.2 and KWCAG 2.2, and produces a report that shows what to fix and how. Automation is only the start: an evaluator adds manual verdicts on top, so the result is fit for real conformance reviews. It is free, and all of the code is public.
about.whyDesc: There are plenty of accessibility checkers. Most cover only the international standard or only the Korean one, and even the good ones leave you writing the actual report from scratch. We wanted a tool that evaluates both standards at once, produces a submission-ready report, and lets anyone inspect the judgment logic in code. So we built it.

org.name: Web managers at organizations
org.desc: See where your site stands in numbers, and hand the report to management or your vendor as a share link or PDF. Turn on scheduled scans and it watches your site daily.

dev.name: Developers and agencies
dev.desc: Every violation comes with the page URL, CSS selector, offending code, and a fix example. Export an AI fix request file and paste it into Claude or ChatGPT to get corrected code.

auditor.name: Accessibility evaluators
auditor.desc: Automation lays the groundwork; anything it can't judge is left as a manual check with testing instructions. Record verdicts and notes to complete a combined automated + manual report.

about.methodDesc: We didn't invent our own criteria. We follow what W3C and the Korean national standard define.

wcagem.name: WCAG-EM
wcagem.desc: W3C's official website evaluation methodology. Scoping, exploration, representative sampling, auditing, and reporting follow this procedure, with export compatible with the W3C Report Tool.

wcag.name: WCAG 2.2
wcag.desc: The international guidelines. Each of the 55 A/AA success criteria is shown as passed, violated, or needs manual review.

kwcag.name: KWCAG 2.2
kwcag.desc: The Korean guidelines (KS X OT0003), presented as the same 33-item matrix used in domestic certification audits.

auto.name: Automated audits
auto.desc: Samples pages from your sitemap and internal links, then runs ~90 axe-core rules: alt text, color contrast, keyboard access — the things a machine can judge with confidence. Pages are re-checked at mobile width, and same-origin iframes are audited too.

workbench.name: Evaluation workbench
workbench.desc: Evaluators record per-criterion verdicts and observations on top of the automated results. Add site details and an executive summary and it becomes the print/PDF report.

extension.name: Chrome extension
extension.desc: Admin screens behind login and bot-blocked sites are out of a server's reach, so the extension audits them right in your browser and merges results into your site report. It also ships on-page tools: color-blindness simulation, focus-order overlay, a contrast eyedropper.

badge.name: Accessibility badge
badge.desc: Verified domains get an SVG badge showing the latest compliance rate, ready to embed.

schedule.name: Scheduled scans and alerts
schedule.desc: Turn it on and your site is scanned daily. If compliance drops or new violations appear, you get an email; the dashboard shows the trend over time.

about.honestyDesc: Automated tools can only find a portion of accessibility issues. We state that limit in the report itself, not in fine print, and mark everything requiring human judgment as a manual check with testing instructions. Our automated results alone cannot support a conformance claim — and you should be skeptical of any tool that says otherwise.
about.openDesc: The code and development history of the scan engine, the WCAG/KWCAG mapping, and the Korean remediation guides are on GitHub. If a verdict looks wrong, check the reasoning there — or propose an improvement.

name.title: The story behind our name
name.a11yDesc: "a11y" is the standard numeronym for accessibility, counting the 11 letters between the a and the y. Read aloud in Korean it becomes "alli" (알리), which happens to sound exactly like the Korean verb for "to make known". Add "chek" (check) and you get our Korean name, 알리첵.
name.part1Desc: The Korean reading of a11y — and the verb 알리다, "to make known". We surface issues that would otherwise stay invisible.
name.part2Desc: Check: audit against the standards and tick issues off one by one, like the checkmark in our logo.
name.slogan: 알리첵 — we make accessibility issues known, and check them off.

about.makerDesc1: This service is built and run by the web manager at Daegu Cyber University.
about.makerDesc2: Daegu Cyber University was founded on the spirit of "Love, Light, and Freedom" and has specialized in special education, rehabilitation, and welfare. When you run the web for a university whose mission is widening the door of learning for students with disabilities, accessibility stops being a compliance item and becomes the obvious thing to do. Allycheck is a tool that makes that obvious thing easy for other organizations and developers too.

impact.title: The impact of A11y Check
impact.desc: A11y Check is a service run for the public good of web accessibility. We publish aggregate metrics of the change it creates — no individual site or user data is included.

improve.title: Verified improvements
improve.desc: When a site is re-audited with fewer violations or a higher compliance rate than its first audit, we count it as a verified improvement.

oss.title: GitHub
oss.desc: The development history of the audit engine and the full Korean-language KWCAG 2.2 rule catalog is on GitHub. Anyone can verify and contribute.

impact.note: Metrics refresh hourly (last computed: {date}). Compliance rates cover the automated scope only — automated tools find part of all accessibility issues.

usage.title: Report usage
usage.desc: Signals that reports are turning into real remediation work.

directory.title: Audited sites
directory.desc: Sites audited with A11y Check that opted in to public listing. Anyone can view each report.
directory.disclaimer: The compliance rate shown is based on automated checks (axe + custom rules) and is not an official web accessibility quality certification. Only sites whose owners verified ownership and chose to list publicly appear here.
directory.empty: No sites have been publicly listed yet.
directory.cta: To audit your own site and issue a badge,

reset.title: Set a new password
reset.desc: Enter the new password you want to use.
reset.done: Password changed. Redirecting to your dashboard…
reset.noSession: The reset link is invalid or expired. Please request a new one from the sign-in page.
reset.errShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
reset.errGeneric: Failed to change. Please try again.

sitemap.title: Sitemap
sitemap.desc: All pages of A11y Check at a glance — another way to find pages besides the top navigation.

serviceStatus.offlineDesc: Please check your network connection. We will resume automatically once you are back online.
serviceStatus.degradedDesc: We are checking for recovery automatically. If it continues, please try again in a moment.

pricing.title: Everything is free for now
pricing.desc: All A11y Check features are currently free. Paid plans for teams and organizations that need higher limits are in preparation — until then, use the limits below freely.

free.name: Free
free.desc: Ongoing checks for personal and small sites
free.extra: All features: reports, badges, scheduled scans, extension

pro.name: Pro
pro.desc: Creators and teams managing multiple sites

pricing.note: Until paid plans launch, every account runs on Free limits. Need more? Contact us and we will review and assign limits individually. Launch timing and prices will be announced on this page.
